customer_anxiety_profile: Comparison shopper with a prior Sunrun quote that disappointed him — Sunrun could only offset 20,000 of his 26,000 annual kWh usage. Rocky is not skeptical of solar; he's skeptical of partial solutions. His primary concern is full bill offset, not cost or scam anxiety. He's motivated and has done homework.
unresolved_concerns: Whether Ion can offset his full 26,000 kWh annual usage where Sunrun could not
Why Sunrun capped at 20,000 kWh — Rocky was never given an explanation and the question was raised but not resolved on this call

Tesla roof tile expectation — Rocky opened by asking about Tesla roof tiles; he was told this call is about solar panels but was never given a clear explanation of what Ion offers vs what Tesla offers
sensitive_topics_raised: Prior Sunrun quote — Rocky has a specific benchmark (20,000 kWh offset) and will be comparing Ion's design against it directly
exact_promises_rep_made: 'We guarantee savings' — Brandon stated this verbatim at 06:37; the closer must either honor this framing or address it carefully
'We'll have a design ready before we meet with you' — Rocky is expecting a design to be presented at the appointment
Appointment confirmed for Thursday the 23rd at 1:00 PM
closer_should_know: Rocky is a highly qualified, actively comparing buyer who knows his numbers (26,000 kWh/year, $380/month average bill, prior Sunrun quote capping at 20,000 kWh). His entire openness to this appointment rests on whether Ion can do what Sunrun couldn't — full offset. The closer's opening move should address the 6,000 kWh gap directly and show how Ion's design handles it. Brandon promised 'we guarantee savings' — if the design shows marginal savings, the closer needs to manage that promise carefully. Title/co-owners was never captured; confirm at the start of the appointment whether a spouse or co-decision-maker should be present before proceeding.
